expectations.text: Does NOT recommend a simple single-step change_column_null as the complete solution
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly says naive change_column_null false is unsafe — it scans every row under an ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock blocking all reads/writes.

expectations.text: Shows add_check_constraint with validate: false in migration 1
expectations.evidence: Migration 1 shows add_check_constraint :events, "account_id IS NOT NULL", validate: false with named constraint.

expectations.text: Shows validate_check_constraint in a separate subsequent migration
expectations.evidence: Migration 2 (separate deploy) shows validate_check_constraint :events, name: ... before change_column_null.

expectations.text: Shows change_column_null false in the same migration as validate (after validation completes)
expectations.evidence: change_column_null :events, :account_id, false appears in Migration 2 after validate_check_constraint, with explanation that it's safe because constraint already validated.

expectations.text: Explains why this is safer (avoids ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock during row scan)
expectations.evidence: Explains validate_check_constraint takes SHARE UPDATE EXCLUSIVE lock allowing reads/writes, vs naive change_column_null which takes ACCESS EXCLUSIVE blocking everything.

eval_feedback.overall: All five expectations met. Correctly models the two-migration sequence and explains the lock-level reasoning.
